title: aster eriophorus
intro: Aster eriophorus, also known as the hairy aster or ball-headed aster, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever. The root and leaves of Aster eriophorus contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions, including colds, coughs, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Aster eriophorus can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being.
